name: Jeremiah
shortDescription: Jeremiah was a major prophet in the Hebrew Bible, known for his prophecies of destruction and eventual restoration of Judah.
description: Jeremiah was a major prophet in the Hebrew Bible, believed to have been active in Jerusalem from the reign of Josiah (c. 626 BC) until after the fall of Jerusalem and the destruction of Solomon's Temple in 587 BC. He is considered one of the most important prophets in Judaism, known for his unflinching condemnation of idolatry, social injustice, and the corruption of the religious establishment. Jeremiah's prophetic ministry was marked by hardship and persecution, as his messages of impending doom were largely rejected by the people of Judah. Despite facing opposition, he remained steadfast in his commitment to delivering God's word, warning of the Babylonian invasion and the eventual exile of the Jewish people. Jeremiah also prophesied about a new covenant that God would establish with his people, one written on their hearts rather than on stone tablets, offering hope for a future restoration. His laments over the destruction of Jerusalem are preserved in the Book of Lamentations.
quests: Warning the people of Judah to repent

sources.title: Who's Who in the Jewish Bible
sources.author: David Mandel

sources.title: The Cambridge Companion to the Hebrew Bible/Old Testament
